title: Spring Claw Care: Transitioning to Warmer Weather
description: A comprehensive guide to adjusting claw maintenance routines as the northern hemisphere moves into late spring.
main_content:
As we enter early May, the northern hemisphere experiences longer days and rising temperatures. This shift affects claw health and growth patterns. 

**Hydration is key**: Warmer weather can dry out claws. Use a moisturizing balm with natural oils (coconut or jojoba) to prevent brittleness. 

**Trim smarter**: Spring growth spurts mean claws may need more frequent trimming. Check every 5-7 days rather than the usual 7-10. 

**Protect from allergens**: Pollen and dust can accumulate under claws. Clean with a gentle brush after outdoor activities. 

**Watch for splitting**: Rapid temperature changes can cause micro-cracks. Apply a strengthening serum weekly. 

**Activity adjustment**: More time outside means claws are naturally filed on rough surfaces. Inspect for uneven wear and file accordingly. 

Remember: healthy claws reflect overall wellness. Pair this routine with a balanced diet rich in biotin and omega-3s.
key_takeaways: Increase claw hydration due to warmer, drier air
Shorten trim intervals to every 5-7 days
Clean claws after outdoor exposure to remove allergens
Apply strengthening products to prevent splitting
Monitor natural wear from increased outdoor activity
